text: Every night, when the sun goes down and the whole world gets quiet, a little boy named Mason gets ready for bed. Mason is six years old, with long black hair that swoops just above his shoulders, and a smile that could light up the darkest room.
image_prompt: cozy bedroom night warm light boy long black hair light skin

text: Mason had the biggest imagination of anyone you would ever meet. While other kids saw a ceiling, Mason saw a sky full of stars. While they saw a bedroom, Mason saw a launchpad. And tonight, he had a feeling something magical was about to happen.
image_prompt: boy at window at night looking at stars dreaming glowing

text: He brushed his teeth, pulled back his rocket-ship covers, and climbed into bed. His mum kissed him on the forehead and whispered — sweet dreams, brave one. And as his eyes got heavier and heavier, the stars outside his window began to twinkle just a little bit brighter.
image_prompt: boy in bed eyes closing cozy warm blanket stars twinkling window

text: And then... something wonderful happened. The bedroom began to shimmer and glow. The walls melted away like smoke. And Mason found himself standing somewhere he had never been before — somewhere incredible. Somewhere out of this world.
image_prompt: magical transformation bedroom dissolving into space glowing swirling dream portal

text: He looked down. He was wearing a brilliant white spacesuit, gleaming silver at the edges, with his name — MASON — printed right across the chest. On his head sat a shining helmet, and through the visor, he could see the entire universe stretching out before him.
image_prompt: boy astronaut white spacesuit name on chest helmet visor stars reflecting

text: BOOM! A great silver rocket appeared behind him, tall as a mountain, blazing with golden fire at the bottom. Mason didn't hesitate — not for even one second. He was brave. He climbed inside, strapped in tight, and the countdown began. Ten. Nine. Eight.
image_prompt: giant silver rocket launch pad golden flames countdown dramatic night sky

text: Three. Two. One. BLAST OFF! The rocket roared like thunder and shot into the sky like a streak of pure light. Mason felt himself pushed back into his seat and he laughed — a big, joyful laugh — as the clouds rushed past him and the sky turned from blue, to purple, to the deep and endless black of space.
image_prompt: rocket blasting through clouds into space trail of fire stars appearing

text: When the rocket slowed, Mason looked out of the window and gasped. There was Earth — his home — a beautiful blue and green marble floating quietly in the dark. He pressed his nose against the glass and whispered — wow. Just — wow.
image_prompt: Earth from space blue green marble clouds swirling beautiful view from rocket window

text: Mason used his smart mind to set his navigation course. He studied the star maps, calculated the distances, and pointed his rocket toward the most interesting blip on his screen — a planet no astronaut had ever visited before. A planet that glowed purple and gold.
image_prompt: astronaut boy studying star map screen glowing controls clever concentrated

text: The purple and gold planet grew bigger and bigger in the window until — THUD — the rocket landed with a gentle bump. Mason pulled on his helmet, took a deep breath, and stepped outside onto the surface of an alien world for the very first time.
image_prompt: alien planet purple gold glowing crystals strange landscape rocket landed boy stepping out

text: The ground sparkled like crushed diamonds. Strange glowing plants swayed in a soft breeze. And up above, not one moon but three moons hung in the sky like giant lanterns, each a different colour — pink, blue, and silver.
image_prompt: alien planet surface sparkling ground glowing plants three coloured moons pink blue silver

text: Then Mason heard a sound. A tiny, scared sound — like a soft whimper from behind a glowing rock. He walked over carefully, knelt down, and found the most extraordinary creature he had ever seen. It was small and round, with enormous purple eyes, tiny wings like a dragonfly, and it was trembling.
image_prompt: small cute alien creature round purple eyes tiny wings trembling glowing rock planet surface

text: Mason was strong — but being strong didn't always mean being loud or tough. Sometimes being strong meant being gentle. He held out his hand very slowly, smiled his big warm smile, and spoke softly. Hello, little friend. I won't hurt you. I promise.
image_prompt: boy astronaut kneeling gently hand outstretched to small cute alien creature warm glow

text: The creature blinked its huge eyes, sniffed his glove, and then — it smiled. And when it smiled, the whole planet lit up in a thousand colours. The glowing plants stretched tall. The three moons shone brighter. And Mason knew he had made a friend.
image_prompt: alien creature smiling planet exploding in colour light rainbow plants glowing moons bright celebration

text: Together they explored the planet. Mason was brave enough to cross a crystal bridge over a purple river. He was smart enough to decode the alien symbols on an ancient stone door. And he was strong enough to push open that great door and discover what was hidden inside — a sky full of captured stars, all waiting to be set free.
image_prompt: crystal bridge purple river ancient stone door glowing symbols astronaut boy exploring

text: Mason reached up and opened the door wide. The stars burst out like a firework, shooting up through the sky — each one finding its perfect place back in the universe where it belonged. His little alien friend squeaked with delight and spun in circles around him.
image_prompt: stars bursting free through open door shooting into night sky galaxy explosion colour joy

text: But even the greatest adventures must come to an end. Mason's rocket gave a gentle beep — it was time to go home. He hugged his little alien friend — the creature made a sound like a tiny song — and Mason promised he would visit again someday.
image_prompt: boy astronaut hugging tiny alien friend rocket glowing warmly goodbye purple planet

text: The rocket lifted off gently this time, as if the planet itself was giving him a soft push home. Mason watched the purple and gold world grow smaller and smaller until it was just a tiny twinkling dot among a billion other stars.
image_prompt: rocket slowly lifting from purple planet floating upward starfield planet getting smaller below

text: He sailed back through the stars, past comets and galaxies and swirling clouds of colour, back toward that beautiful blue and green marble — back toward home. He smiled all the way. The smile of someone who had been somewhere extraordinary and brought a little bit of that magic back with them.
image_prompt: rocket sailing through galaxy comets colourful nebula heading toward Earth glowing warm

text: And then, as gently as it had begun, the dream began to fade. The rocket, the stars, the alien friend — all of it softened and shimmered. And Mason found himself back in his bedroom, tucked under his rocket-ship covers, the stars outside his window still twinkling quietly.
image_prompt: cozy bedroom night boy sleeping peacefully stars twinkling window dream fading warm light

text: He was brave. He was smart. He was strong. And he was six years old, with long black hair and the biggest imagination in the whole universe. Tomorrow the adventure would wait. But for now — Mason smiled in his sleep. And the stars outside smiled back.
image_prompt: boy sleeping peacefully happy smile cozy bed stars glowing softly outside window night magical
